steps.title: Identify the gap between expectation and discovery
steps.content:
Researchers believed this defensive ultrasound was exclusive to a few specific moth groups.

Yet Barber et al. tested 252 genera across most large-bodied moth families and found 52 genera produced these ultrasonic clicks—including multiple geometer genera not previously documented to have this defense.

The signal: the study discovered this ability in far more moth groups than previously known.

steps.title: Apply to find the correct inference
steps.content:
A discovery of 52 genera possessing this defense—when researchers had limited it to just a handful—directly suggests the defense is more widespread than previously thought.

Choice D captures this: the defense “may be a more prevalent defense strategy among large-bodied moths than previously known to researchers.” The other three either introduce unsupported elements (alternate uses in B, other strategies in C) or reverse the logic (A claims most moths lack this defense).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
